meta.title: Provider Flavors + Harness Roadmap
meta.description: A visual roadmap showing what Leviathan ports, wraps, extracts, and rejects from OMX, OMC, Hermes, OpenClaw, and ClawHIP.

props.title: Provider Flavors + Harness Roadmap
props.subtitle: Port the neutral contracts. Wrap the provider shells. Extract the high-signal patterns. Reject provider truth and product sprawl.
props.meta: Derived from the April 5 synthesis + current proposal artifact

props.label: DECISION MODEL
props.title: How each candidate feature gets judged
props.subtitle: This is the one-line rule set that keeps planning coherent: neutral Lev-owned contracts go in core, provider-shaped shells stay wrapped, useful patterns get extracted, and provider truth stays out.

props.content: The architectural filter is the owner map already in the repo: FlowMind owns control and policy, Orchestration owns deterministic execution, Graph owns state and knowledge, Event Bus owns causality and lifecycle, Core Platforms owns provider adapters, and Poly remains binder and codegen only. Anything that does not place cleanly into one of those owners is not ready to enter core.

rows.meaning: Bring in as a neutral Lev-owned contract or reusable runtime capability.
Support as a provider flavor or runtime shell without adopting its worldview.
Lift the pattern or contract without taking the implementation or product scope.
Keep out of canon because it would create provider truth, ontology drift, or product sprawl.

props.label: FEATURE BUFFET
props.title: What we are taking from each ecosystem source
props.subtitle: One-liners only: enough to review, tweak, and approve before any implementation slice starts.

props.title: 🛰️ OpenClaw
props.content:
**Take:** gateway-as-control-plane framing; typed WS + HTTP edge; onboarding flow shape; doctor / repair / migration UX; auth / tool-policy / sandbox posture; remote ops patterns like trusted proxy, tailscale/SSH, and multi-gateway warnings.

**Use posture:** wrap as a gateway/control-plane flavor; extract trusted ingress + operator playbooks; reject assistant / channel / app product breadth as core ontology.

props.title: 🧭 Hermes
props.content:
**Take:** first-run setup wizard shape; `migrate` as a product surface; actionable `doctor` / `status`; non-TTY-safe wizard behavior; bounded memory-provider slot; profile-scoped runtime identity; delegation semantics; ACP/editor packaging ideas.

**Use posture:** port onboarding/runtime ceremony; wrap as an external flavor when useful; reject `~/.hermes` as truth and Hermes MCP server as Lev’s spine.

props.title: ⚙️ OMX
props.content:
**Take:** tmux team lifecycle (`spawn / status / await / resume / shutdown`); machine-facing team and mailbox semantics; session/hook envelope; background HUD/watch surface; mixed worker CLI selection; project-scoped runtime isolation patterns.

**Use posture:** port neutral tmux lifecycle semantics into Lev-owned execution/runtime contracts; wrap runtime isolation through harness/config; reject `.omx` mutation and `.omx` as truth.

props.title: 👥 OMC
props.content:
**Take:** staged team pipeline (`team-plan -> team-prd -> team-exec -> team-verify -> team-fix`); worker shell / operator UX; worker health / restart / status ergonomics; tmux CLI worker model; HUD/statusline patterns; lifecycle hook shell around long-running orchestration.

**Use posture:** wrap as a provider shell in Core Platforms; extract worker UX and operator shell ideas; reject Claude-specific runtime worldview and `.omc` as authority.

props.title: 📣 ClawHIP
props.content:
**Take:** normalized `session.*` provider-edge contract; hook-envelope routing doctrine; renderer/sink split; tmux/session notification-edge patterns; Discord/Slack edge delivery routing.

**Use posture:** port the edge contract and hook doctrine; extract renderer/sink split; reject the transport-specific daemon as a runtime center.

props.label: PHASES
props.title: The actual build order
props.subtitle: P0 is only neutral contracts Lev lacks. P1 wraps provider flavors after the contracts exist. P2 is wording and polish after runtime ownership is stable.

items.title: tmux team lifecycle contract
items.description: Owner: core/orchestration + core/daemon + core/harness. Source posture: mostly OMX port with Lev-owned naming.

items.title: provider-edge `session.*` normalization
items.description: Owner: core/event-bus + plugins/core-platforms. Source posture: ClawHIP + OMX-inspired contract port.

items.title: setup / migrate / doctor / status ceremony
items.description: Owner: onboarding / harness / runtime shell surfaces. Source posture: Hermes + OpenClaw port/extract.

items.title: wrap provider flavors
items.description: OMC worker shell, Hermes bounded composition, OpenClaw gateway flavor — all thin wrappers over Lev-owned contracts.

items.title: wording and operator polish
items.description: Selective prompt corpus lifts, HUD/status polish, release/operator delight — only after P0/P1 are stable.

props.label: NEXT
props.title: How we execute from here
props.subtitle: The proposal is the roadmap truth. Work remains the entry surface. Stack is only the execution helper after you approve a slice.

props.title: ✅ Recommended first slice
props.content:
**P0-A — tmux team lifecycle contract**

Why first: it closes the clearest real gap, turns Harness IR into something concrete, makes later provider flavors easier to wrap cleanly, and avoids pulling onboarding/gateway breadth into the first build step.

1. Proposal remains canonical
2. You approve one slice
3. I create one child plan/spec for that slice
4. If needed: plan-align-gates-proposal or sdlc-deepen-plan
5. Then: sdlc-exec-validate
6. No multi-slice execution unless genuinely independent
